notes.anchor: deposit my accounts, made up under the Julian Law, with two cities
notes.note: Caesar's lex Iulia de repetundis (59 BC) required a departing governor to lodge two copies of his official accounts in the chief cities of his province before forwarding a third to the treasury at Rome; Cicero deposits his at Laodicea and a second city.

The etesiae were the steady north-northwest summer winds of the Aegean; blowing dead against a westbound voyage, they regularly stalled ships sailing from Asia Minor toward Greece and threatened to delay Cicero's homeward journey.

A supplicatio was a public thanksgiving voted by the Senate, days of festival at the temples in a commander's honour; Cicero had won one for his Cilician victory and gauges the goodwill of the new magistrates by their support for it.
